strategy.paper_title: A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation
strategy.methodology_text:
Faber's quantitative tactical-asset-allocation model evaluates each asset's monthly close against its 10-month simple moving average. If the close is above the SMA, the position is fully invested; if below, the position is moved to cash (T-bills in the paper). The model is applied across five asset classes (US equity, foreign equity, bonds, commodities, REITs) in an equal-weighted portfolio, rebalanced monthly.

v1 Archimedes adaptation: 10 trading months ≈ 210 daily bars; we use a 200-day SMA for tractability on daily data. Single-asset implementation — portfolio-level weighting across operations is the responsibility of upstream allocation logic. Cash leg returns are modeled as zero (conservative); the paper assumes 90-day T-bill yield.
